growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: bacdive-microa.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

sec.units: ppm (headspace, from 5 mM L-methionine, 16 h; negligible)
sec.method: gas chromatography with flame photometric detector

growth_records.cite: Hara, Takeshi et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer fuels the methionine metabolism of Fusobacterium nucleatum to stimulate volatile methyl mercaptan production. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00764-23. [PMC10878106]
growth_records.snippet: monocultures of S. gordonii WT ... showed depleted arginine, as well as release of 1.2 ... mM ornithine ... (P < 0.01). ... Streptococcus gordonii DL1 ... L-Methionine 0.36 ± 0.11 (ppm CH3SH).
growth_records.notes: Resting washed-cell suspension (OD600=1.0), not a growth culture (no net OD increase; mu~0, no doubling time). Ornithine secretion: 1.2 mM released over the 12 h time-course (Fig 3); rate = 1.2 mM / 12 h = 0.10 mM/h. Arginine (1.0 mM in mCDM) depleted via ArcD arginine-ornithine antiporter (no per-hour value stated). CH3SH negligible (0.36+/-0.11 ppm, Table 1, 5 mM Met, 16 h). No gDW-per-OD conversion given so mmol/gDW/h not derivable.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM) with glucose and lactic acid

up.method: Monod affinity constant K_S; no volumetric uptake rate (mmol/gDW/h) reported in this paper

sec.method: Main fermentation product (qualitative, from cited literature/proposed stoichiometry); no rate measured

yields.Yxs: computed thermodynamically (max theoretical growth yield vs electron donor per Heijnen-Kleerebezem method); not an experimental value

growth_records.cite: Sangha JS et al. Advancing dental biofilm models: the integral role of pH in predicting S. mutans colonization. mSphere (2024). DOI:10.1128/msphere.00743-24. [PMC11774048]
growth_records.snippet: Streptococcus gordonii Glucose 0.492 6.6 1.88 ... S. gordonii, S. mutans, and A. oris are facultative anaerobes that are assumed to consume glucose ... and produce lactic acid as the main product
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 0.492 h-1 (Table 2), K_S = 6.6 mmol/L (=1.88 g/L glucose). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.492 = 1.409 h. mu_max and K_S are experimentally derived kinetic parameters CITED FROM Sangha JS et al. 2024, Microbiol Spectr 12:e0371323, doi:10.1128/spectrum.03713-23 (ref 17). This paper (PMC11774048) is an in silico individual-based/ODE modeling study; no new measured uptake/secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h are reported. Products qualitative only.

up.method: Max acetaldehyde at 2000 mM ethanol; C1/2 = 605.0 +/- 316.4 mM

sec.method: Vmax by GC on resting washed cells at 2000 mM ethanol; +/- 14.8

growth_records.cite: Tagaino R et al. Bifacial biological effects of ethanol: acetaldehyde production by oral Streptococcus species and the antibacterial effects of ethanol against these bacteria. Journal of Oral Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.1080/20002297.2021.1937884. [PMC8204988]
growth_records.notes: Resting-cell assay. Acetaldehyde Vmax=41.1 umol/mg wet wt/min at 2000 mM ethanol; C1/2=605.0 mM. Doubling time increased gradually with ethanol 0-500 mM for S. gordonii.

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 14791 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/14791

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
